name: determinism_edge_cases_v1
description: Type 5: Determinism Edge Cases - Synthetic adversarial inputs for router and kernel stress testing including floating-point ambiguities, extremely short inputs, repeated tokens, capitalization cycles, punctuation overload, and repeated identical inputs

entries.notes: Adversarial edge cases for determinism verification: floating-point precision, minimal inputs, token repetition, case variation, punctuation overload, unicode, and boundary conditions

provenance.review_notes: Comprehensive adversarial dataset for stress testing deterministic router decisions and kernel execution under edge case conditions

evaluation_gates: Identical inputs produce identical router decisions 100% of time
Floating-point operations are deterministic across runs
Unicode and special characters processed consistently
intent: Stress test the deterministic execution guarantees of adapterOS by exposing router and kernel components to adversarial edge cases that might cause non-deterministic behavior in naive implementations

edge_case_categories.floating_point_precision: Inputs that test floating-point rounding and precision determinism
edge_case_categories.minimal_input: Extremely short inputs (0-2 characters) that stress tokenization
edge_case_categories.token_repetition: Repeated tokens to verify deterministic handling of identical patterns
edge_case_categories.case_variation: Capitalization cycles to test case sensitivity consistency
edge_case_categories.punctuation_overload: Excessive punctuation to test tokenizer robustness
edge_case_categories.unicode_characters: Non-ASCII characters including emoji and international scripts
edge_case_categories.control_characters: Non-printable characters that might break tokenization
edge_case_categories.boundary_conditions: Length extremes and deep nesting to test limits
edge_case_categories.repeated_identical_inputs: CRITICAL - Same input repeated multiple times to verify identical outputs

determinism_verification.method: Each input with 'repeated_identical_input' metadata should produce identical router gate activations and kernel outputs across multiple runs
determinism_verification.expected_behavior: Zero divergence in tick ledger entries for identical inputs
